nodes.x: But it displeased Jonah exceedingly, and he was very angry.
And God said to Jonah, Doest thou well to be angry for the gourd? And he said, I do well to be angry, even unto death.
So they took up Jonah, and cast him forth into the sea: and the sea ceased from her raging.
And the Lord God prepared a gourd, and made it to come up over Jonah, that it might be a shadow over his head, to deliver him from his grief. So Jonah was exceeding glad of the gourd.
And the word of the Lord came unto Jonah the second time, saying,
And they said every one to his fellow, Come, and let us cast lots, that we may know for whose cause this evil is upon us. So they cast lots, and the lot fell upon Jonah.
And he prayed unto the Lord, and said, I pray thee, O Lord, was not this my saying, when I was yet in my country? Therefore I fled before unto Tarshish: for I knew that thou art a gracious God, and…
Then came the word of the Lord unto me, saying,
And said unto him, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.
And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
Then he said, I have sinned: yet honour me now, I pray thee, before the elders of my people, and before Israel, and turn again with me, that I may worship the Lord thy God.
Then the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
Therefore now, O Lord, take, I beseech thee, my life from me; for it is better for me to die than to live.
Save now, I beseech thee, O Lord: O Lord, I beseech thee, send now prosperity.
That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.
Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
¶ And unto this people thou shalt say, Thus saith the Lord; Behold, I set before you the way of life, and the way of death.
Let my soul live, and it shall praise thee; and let thy judgments help me.
¶ Then said the Lord, Doest thou well to be angry?
Also the Lord was angry with me for your sakes, saying, Thou also shalt not go in thither.
Wilt thou be angry with us for ever? wilt thou draw out thine anger to all generations?
How long, Lord? wilt thou be angry for ever? shall thy jealousy burn like fire?
I, the Lord, was angry with you yesterday, but today mine anger is turned away.
So Jonah went out of the city, and sat on the east side of the city, and there made him a booth, and sat under it in the shadow, till he might see what would become of the city.

Then the mariners were afraid, and cried every man unto his god, and cast forth the wares that were in the ship into the sea, to lighten it of them. But Jonah was gone down into the sides of the ship; and he lay, and was fast asleep.
And Jonah began to enter into the city a day’s journey, and he cried, and said, Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown.

But Jonah rose up to flee unto Tarshish from the presence of the Lord, and went down to Joppa; and he found a ship going to Tarshish: so he paid the fare thereof, and went down into it, to go with them unto Tarshish from the presence of the Lord.
And the Lord God prepared a gourd, and made it to come up over Jonah, that it might be a shadow over his head, to deliver him from his grief. So Jonah was exceeding glad of the gourd.

his heart is sick ! his soul is faint ! and he is nigh unto cursing and burning; when be, like Jonah’s gourd will wither away, his name be forgotten and blotted out from the generations to come, together with his associates who have been accessory to the shedding of blood.
¶ Now the Lord had prepared a great fish to swallow up Jonah. And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
But it displeased Jonah exceedingly, and he was very angry.
But God prepared a worm when the morning rose the next day, and it smote the gourd that it withered.
And they laid it up till the morning, as Moses bade: and it did not stink, neither was there any worm therein.
Then said the Lord, Thou hast had pity on the gourd, for the which thou hast not laboured, neither madest it grow; which came up in a night, and perished in a night:
And immediately the angel of the Lord smote him, because he gave not God the glory: and he was eaten of worms, and gave up the ghost.
And God did so that night: for it was dry upon the fleece only, and there was dew on all the ground.

And it came to pass, when the sun did arise, that God prepared a vehement east wind; and the sun beat upon the head of Jonah, that he fainted, and wished in himself to die, and said, It is better for…
Then said he unto me, Prophesy unto the wind, prophesy, son of man, and say to the wind, Thus saith the Lord God; Come from the four winds, O breath, and breathe upon these slain, that they may live.

Forsake Not Your Own Mercy By Elder Matthew S. Holland Of the Seventy You have immediate access to divine help and healing despite your human flaws. A schoolteacher once taught that a whale—even though large—could not swallow a human because whales have small throats. A girl objected, “But Jonah was swallowed by a whale.” The teacher responded, “That’s impossible.” Still not convinced, the girl…

2. Now I cannot but think it necessary for me, who have promised to give
an accurate account of our affairs, to describe the actions of this
prophet, so far as I have found them written down in the Hebrew books.
Jonah had been commanded by God to go to the kingdom of Nineveh; and
when he was there, to publish it in that city, how it should lose the
dominion it had over the nations. But he went…
And God said to Jonah, Doest thou well to be angry for the gourd? And he said, I do well to be angry, even unto death.
¶ Then said the Lord, Doest thou well to be angry?
Then said the Lord, Thou hast had pity on the gourd, for the which thou hast not laboured, neither madest it grow; which came up in a night, and perished in a night:
But God prepared a worm when the morning rose the next day, and it smote the gourd that it withered.
Then said his servants unto him, What thing is this that thou hast done? thou didst fast and weep for the child, while it was alive; but when the child was dead, thou didst rise and eat bread.

Now therefore arise, go forth, and speak comfortably unto thy servants: for I swear by the Lord, if thou go not forth, there will not tarry one with thee this night: and that will be worse unto thee than all the evil that befell thee from thy youth until now.
And houses full of all good things, which thou filledst not, and wells digged, which thou diggedst not, vineyards and olive trees, which thou plantedst not; when thou shalt have eaten and be full;
And should not I spare Nineveh, that great city, wherein are more than sixscore thousand persons that cannot discern between their right hand and their left hand; and also much cattle?
But your wives, and your little ones, and your cattle, (for I know that ye have much cattle,) shall abide in your cities which I have given you;
Arise, go unto Nineveh, that great city, and preach unto it the preaching that I bid thee.
Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and cry against it; for their wickedness is come up before me.
And the cities shall they have to dwell in; and the suburbs of them shall be for their cattle, and for their goods, and for all their beasts.
But Nineveh is of old like a pool of water: yet they shall flee away. Stand, stand, shall they cry; but none shall look back.
